user_query:
I want a small, reproducible analysis of my practice discipline and song timing stability so I can focus my work where it matters. Please implement and run a command-line script named analyze_practice.py that reads the three CSVs under input/ and writes results under output/.

Inputs (already in the workspace):
- input/practice_log.csv
- input/songs_catalog.csv
- input/song_bpm_measurements.csv

Requirements:
1) Filtering
- Only include rows with valid == 1 in all files.
- For practice_log.csv, also include only take_type == "practice" (exclude warmups).

2) Practice summary (output/practice_summary.csv)
- Group by exercise and compute: total_minutes (sum of minutes), takes (count of included rows), avg_bpm (mean), median_bpm (median), max_bpm (max), total_errors (sum of errors), and avg_errors_per_min (total_errors / total_minutes).
- Round all numeric fields to 2 decimal places.
- Sort by avg_errors_per_min descending; if tied, sort by exercise name ascending.

3) Song timing summary (output/song_timing_summary.csv)
- Join song_bpm_measurements with songs_catalog by song.
- For each song, over all valid bars from all its takes, compute: mean_bpm, std_bpm (population standard deviation over valid bar BPMs), mean_abs_deviation (mean absolute difference |bpm - target_bpm|), timing_risk_score = std_bpm + mean_abs_deviation, bars_count (number of valid bars), takes_count (number of distinct take_id values that have at least one valid bar).
- Include columns: song, target_bpm, mean_bpm, std_bpm, mean_abs_deviation, timing_risk_score, bars_count, takes_count.
- Round numeric fields to 2 decimal places.
- Sort by timing_risk_score descending; if tied, sort by song name ascending.

4) Focus shortlists
- From practice_summary.csv, write the top 3 exercises by avg_errors_per_min to output/top_exercises_by_errors.csv with columns: exercise, avg_errors_per_min, total_minutes.
- From song_timing_summary.csv, write the top 3 songs by timing_risk_score to output/top_songs_by_timing_risk.csv with columns: song, timing_risk_score, std_bpm, mean_abs_deviation.
- Keep the same rounding and tie-breaking rules.

5) CLI and determinism
- The script must be runnable as:
  python analyze_practice.py --practice input/practice_log.csv --catalog input/songs_catalog.csv --measurements input/song_bpm_measurements.csv --outdir output/
- Create the output/ directory if it doesn’t exist.

Deliverables to produce:
- output/practice_summary.csv
- output/song_timing_summary.csv
- output/top_exercises_by_errors.csv
- output/top_songs_by_timing_risk.csv

I’m a veteran guitarist who believes in grinding it out, so I want this to be something I can re-run anytime after a session. Make sure all computations match the rules above so I can track my progress consistently. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
